meaning: adj.穿制服的,穿规定服装的
examples: A beautiful lady, richly clothed, followed Hugh, and after her came several liveried servants.
The tea was served to guests by liveried footmen.
The thousand liveried angels—begin to cast their beam on the virgin's outward shape—that's her body, the temple that houses her virginal mind.
They were not only beautifully liveried in white, red and gold and Brunswick (later Stobart) green.
At Delhi's swanky Apollo Indraprastha Hospital, liveried guards stand ready to open the doors of arriving cars.
